topic: §4.2.2 c5 — adversarial review of EXP-275 Tier 1 coherence-rule paragraph (queue 289)
our_argument_summary: EXP-275 inserted a single paragraph between paragraphs 5 and 6 of §4.2.2 to address c3 H2 (incomplete operational-rule framing — ISS-1672). The new paragraph quotes the dome’s predictions.html Tier 1 falsification criterion (“No coherent signal at ≥3 stations with r > 0.5 on verified Kp<2 day”), argues it does not rescue the misstatement (still treats globe as null pattern; BOU-2017 already exhibited Chapman-pattern coherence with the August 2017 eclipse contact times), and asserts the BOU-2017 trilemma below holds whichever published rule the dome elects to defend. The c3 hole was substantively closed: the defender retreat to the predictions.html three-tier protocol is now closed by the dome’s own published rule. Verdict (Refuted by Data) holds. The new paragraph is byte-correctly inserted; the rest of the section is byte-identical to the c4-reviewed text plus a separate “acceptance window” → “falsification window” minor polish that landed alongside.

tier1_coherence_rule_quote_verified.live_text_at_line_125: Falsification (Tier 1): No coherent signal at ≥3 stations with r > 0.5 on verified Kp<2 day.
tier1_coherence_rule_quote_verified.section_quote_text: No coherent signal at ≥3 stations with r > 0.5 on verified Kp<2 day
tier1_coherence_rule_quote_verified.match: exact (modulo “Falsification (Tier 1):” prefix, which the section paraphrases as “separate Tier 1 falsification criterion”)
tier1_coherence_rule_quote_verified.verdict: quote is verbatim and current. The c3 evidence (line 125) survives.

globe_prediction_quote_verified.live_text_at_line_113: Globe prediction: 0.0 nT systematic (or ionospheric Sq disruption — competing mechanism exists, see WIN-068 honest caveat).

line_proximity_check.verdict:
Lines 113 and 125 are DIFFERENT

elements separated by station priority table and E-PRED-B paragraph. Both within the TIER 1 — Magnetic Signal (Globe-Compatible) h3 block. The new EXP-275 paragraph asserts they appear on “the same predictions.html line” — this is factually inaccurate; they are on the same Tier 1 block but not the same line.

dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Quotes two verbatim strings from predictions.html (Tier 1 coherence rule line 125; Globe prediction line 113) and asserts a relationship (“same line”) that does not hold byte-literally.
data_sources_used: BOU 2017 magnetometer datum (-10.9 nT) cited via §4.2.5 internal link
August 2017 eclipse path (US, traversed Boulder area) — cited as historical precedent for Chapman-pattern coherence
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: The paragraph CLOSES one defender retreat (the predictions.html three-tier protocol as alternate operational rule) but leaves a smaller defender retreat OPEN (single-station BOU-2017 vs the rule’s ≥3-station coverage requirement — see Hole 2 below).
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies:
“the same predictions.html line publishing the rule also publishes ‘Globe prediction: 0.0 nT systematic’” — inaccurate. The two strings appear on different

elements (lines 113 and 125), separated by a station-priority table and the E-PRED-B paragraph. They are within the same Tier 1 magnetic h3 block, not the same line.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Section-level prose, not a per-WIN code review. The eclipse WIN cluster (WIN-010 etc.) carries monitoring tags individually.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Section-level argument, not a relabel claim.

holes_found.description:
FACTUAL MISSTATEMENT — the new EXP-275 paragraph parenthetical reads “the same predictions.html line publishing the rule also publishes ‘Globe prediction: 0.0 nT systematic’”. Verified against a fresh fetch of predictions.html on 2026-04-30: the Tier 1 coherence rule lives in the

at line 125, while ‘Globe prediction: 0.0 nT systematic’ lives in a separate

at line 113 — 12 lines apart, separated by a station-priority table and the E-PRED-B paragraph. They are co-located in the same TIER 1 — Magnetic Signal (Globe-Compatible) h3 block, but they are NOT on the same line. A dome defender could exploit this: ‘check the source — lines 113 and 125 are visibly different

elements; your refutation rests on a factual misread of where my page publishes what.’ The argument survives the correction (both strings are in the same Tier 1 block, so the structural point holds), but the literal phrasing as written is wrong and gives a defender a free rhetorical opening.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace “the same predictions.html line publishing the rule” with “the same Tier 1 magnetic block of predictions.html publishing the rule” (or simply “the same Tier 1 block”). Three-word fix; preserves the structural argument; eliminates the factual exploit. ISS-NEW recommended.

holes_found.description: INCOMPLETE COHERENCE-RULE COLLAPSE — the dome’s Tier 1 coherence rule explicitly requires ‘coherent signal at ≥3 stations with r > 0.5’. The new paragraph’s preempting evidence is single-station BOU-2017 temporal coherence with the 2017 contact times. A sophisticated dome defender can retreat: ‘BOU-2017 alone was not a ≥3-station r > 0.5 coherence test — it was one station’s temporal alignment with eclipse contact times. The rule’s discriminator is multi-station spatial-temporal coherence under Kp<2; nothing about a single 2017 station establishes that the August 2026 multi-station test is non-discriminating against Chapman.’ The retreat does not save the dome (Chapman ALSO predicts multi-station coherence for the appropriate path geometry, and the cited Meza et al. 2021 / 207-observation literature includes multi-station 2017 data), but the new paragraph does not pre-empt this move — it relies entirely on BOU-2017 which is single-station.
holes_found.recommendation: Either (a) add a single sentence noting that the Meza et al. 2021 multi-station INTERMAGNET ensemble (cited two paragraphs earlier) ALREADY documents ≥3-station Chapman-pattern coherence for total eclipses including August 2017, so the rule’s multi-station threshold is the very signature standard physics produces; or (b) soften ‘BOU 2017’s −10.9 nT signal already exhibited Chapman-pattern coherence’ to ‘the multi-station 2017 INTERMAGNET ensemble (cited above) already exhibited the very Chapman-pattern ≥3-station coherence the rule asks the 2026 measurement to lack’. Option (a) is a one-sentence add; option (b) re-uses the 207-observation citation already in the section. ISS-NEW recommended.

holes_found.description: UNCITED EMPIRICAL CLAIM — ‘BOU 2017’s −10.9 nT signal already exhibited Chapman-pattern coherence with the August 2017 eclipse contact times’ is a specific empirical claim about temporal coherence with C1/C4 contact times. §4.2.5 only describes BOU 2017 as ‘a Chapman-mechanism measurement’ — a weaker characterization. The two ScienceDirect citations earlier in the section (207 observations / multi-station INTERMAGNET 4 total eclipses) likely include the data underwriting this claim, but the connection is not made explicit in the new paragraph. A defender could ask: ‘where in the literature is BOU 2017’s temporal coherence with contact times specifically documented?’ The answer is presumably the same INTERMAGNET corpus, but the paragraph doesn’t say so.
holes_found.recommendation: Add a parenthetical citation back-pointer: ‘BOU 2017’s −10.9 nT signal already exhibited Chapman-pattern coherence with the August 2017 eclipse contact times (documented in the INTERMAGNET ensemble cited above and in §4.2.5)’. Optional polish; not blocking on its own, but combined with Hole 2 a single Hole-2 fix could absorb this.

holes_found.description: OVERCLAIM — ‘The BOU-2017 trilemma below holds whichever published rule the dome elects to defend.’ The trilemma below is ±3 nT-amplitude-specific in its arithmetic (the ‘|−10.9| / 3 ≈ 3.6’ step is meaningless under the coherence rule). The trilemma’s STRUCTURAL collapse to ‘either accept the rule and concede, or revise and concede the prediction was never discriminating’ holds under either rule, but the arithmetic-form trilemma the reader actually encounters one paragraph later is rule-specific. A pedant defender could note the formal mismatch.
holes_found.recommendation: Soften to ‘The BOU-2017 trilemma below collapses to the same essential admission — accept the rule and concede or revise it and admit the prediction was never discriminating — whichever published rule the dome elects to defend.’ Or remove the sentence; the paragraph already establishes the coherence-rule collapse without needing to assert the trilemma below also generalizes. Optional polish.

holes_found.description: PHRASING COMPLEXITY — ‘the very signature the predictions.html rule asks the August 2026 measurement to lack’ is doubly negated (the rule defines falsification as ABSENCE of coherent signal, so the dome predicts PRESENCE; the paragraph says the rule asks the measurement to lack the signature). A casual TLDR-only reader who clicks through and skims will trip on the parsing. The argument is correct but the wording is opaque.
holes_found.recommendation: Re-word to ‘the very signature the predictions.html rule treats absence of as falsification — BOU 2017 already showed it.’ Or invert positively: ‘the very Chapman-pattern coherence the dome must observe in 2026 to avoid falsification was already present in BOU 2017.’ Polish only.

stronger_arguments.description: The dome’s Tier 1 coherence rule contains its own quiet self-contradiction that this section could exploit instead of (or in addition to) the BOU-2017 angle: line 110 of predictions.html publishes ‘Prediction: −17 to −21 nT umbra-locked ΔB at ≥3 priority stations’ — i.e., the dome PREDICTS amplitudes 17–21 nT, which fall WITHIN the Chapman 5–25 nT range cited two paragraphs earlier. So the dome’s OWN amplitude prediction is consistent with what Chapman would predict. The dome’s discriminator is therefore the contact-phase TIMING (C1/C4 peaks vs totality maximum), not the amplitude. The current section spends most of its argument on amplitude (±3 nT vs 5–25 nT) but the dome’s actual unique claim is timing. Adding a single sentence acknowledging ‘the dome’s amplitude prediction (-17 to -21 nT) is itself within the Chapman 5–25 nT band; the dome’s sole discriminator is contact-phase timing’ sharpens the section without weakening it.
sources: predictions.html line 110: ‘Prediction: −17 to −21 nT umbra-locked ΔB at ≥3 priority stations’
predictions.html line 107: ‘Contact-phase prediction: Signal peaks at C1 (first external contact) and C4 (last external contact) — NOT at totality maximum.’

stronger_arguments.description: Cross-WIN consistency: WIN-068 (cited in the dome’s parenthetical as the ‘honest caveat’ documenting Sq disruption as competing mechanism) is itself a dome WIN claiming the Kim & Chang 2018 ensemble pattern as a confirmed prediction — worth noting that the dome’s TIER 1 prediction line on predictions.html line 111 also says ‘Pattern: Y↑, X↓, Z↓, F↓ matching Kim & Chang 2018 ensemble (WIN-068)’. So the dome explicitly maps its prediction onto a peer-reviewed paper’s ensemble pattern. If our review of WIN-068 establishes Kim & Chang 2018 is itself a Chapman-mechanism paper, the new paragraph could note that the dome is asking the 2026 measurement to confirm an ensemble pattern that, by the dome’s own attribution, is sourced from the standard physics literature.
sources: predictions.html line 111: ‘Pattern: Y↑, X↓, Z↓, F↓ matching Kim & Chang 2018 ensemble (WIN-068)’

kernel_of_truth.description: The new paragraph is genuinely valuable: it pre-empts a real defender retreat (the three-tier protocol on predictions.html as the ‘actual’ operational rule) and quotes the dome’s actual published falsification criterion verbatim. The structural argument — that BOTH the live.html ±3 nT rule AND the predictions.html coherence rule treat the globe as predicting a null pattern, and Chapman predicts a non-null pattern that BOU-2017 historically produced — is correct and lands. The dome author deserves credit for publishing multiple falsification criteria in different places; our review now matches that completeness by addressing both.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The structural argument holds. The factual misstatement (‘same line’) and the single-station vs ≥3-station incompleteness are fixable without disturbing the structural collapse. The verdict (Refuted by Data) is unchanged.

reasoning_for_recommendation: EXP-275 closed the c3 H2 hole substantively — the dome defender retreat to the predictions.html three-tier protocol is now closed by the dome’s own published rule. The verdict and the section’s overall argument are unaffected. Two moderate findings (Hole 1: ‘same line’ misstatement; Hole 2: single-station vs ≥3-station coherence-rule collapse) are surgical fixes — a 3-word change for Hole 1, a 1-sentence add for Hole 2 — that close the remaining defender openings. File issues for these; do NOT block the section, the c3 fix landed.

advocate_mode.strongest_defender_response_to_new_parag:
‘Your paragraph misstates the structure of my predictions.html: line 113 (Globe prediction) and line 125 (Tier 1 falsification) are separate

elements with a station-priority table between them. Your refutation also conflates a single-station BOU-2017 temporal coherence with my rule’s explicit ≥3-station r > 0.5 multi-station criterion. On both counts the rebuttal is sloppy with the source.’
advocate_mode.defender_response_collapses_when: (a) the ‘same line’ phrasing is corrected to ‘same Tier 1 block’ (Hole 1 fix); (b) the BOU-2017 single-station preempt is augmented by a one-sentence pointer to the Meza et al. 2021 multi-station INTERMAGNET ensemble (Hole 2 fix). With both fixes the defender has no remaining rhetorical opening on the new paragraph itself.
advocate_mode.structural_argument_holds_independent_of: Yes — the dome publishes ‘Globe prediction: 0.0 nT systematic’ alongside the coherence rule in the SAME Tier 1 block. The structural critique (rule treats globe as predicting null pattern; Chapman predicts non-null) is correct regardless of the line-proximity wording.

cross_win_consistency.win_068_alignment: Compatible — WIN-068 is the ‘honest caveat’ the dome cites for Sq disruption being a competing mechanism. Section paragraph 5 already quotes the WIN-068 caveat verbatim from predictions.html. The new EXP-275 paragraph builds on this without contradicting WIN-068’s framing.
cross_win_consistency.win_010_alignment: Compatible — §4.2.2 is the eclipse argument cluster anchored by WIN-010. The new paragraph extends but does not re-scope the eclipse-cluster verdict.
cross_win_consistency.section_4_2_5_alignment: Compatible but slightly under-leveraged — §4.2.5 calls BOU 2017 ‘a Chapman-mechanism measurement’. The new paragraph asserts a stronger claim (‘Chapman-pattern coherence with contact times’) without back-pointer; see Hole 3.
cross_win_consistency.section_4_2_3_alignment: Compatible — the hedging-structure argument in §4.2.3 is unchanged; the new paragraph is upstream and parallel.

tag (E-PRED-B paragraph at line 124), 8-row station priority table (lines 114–123),

text_fingerprint.verdict_chip_target: Refuted by Data (eclipse argument cluster — WIN-010)

deeper_analysis:
The integration is structurally correct — EXP-275 inserted exactly the paragraph the c3 H2 recommendation called for, in the location the analyst targeted (between paragraphs 5 and 6). The c3 hole was the absence of acknowledgement of the predictions.html three-tier protocol; the new paragraph quotes it, paraphrases its falsification clause, and argues why the retreat doesn’t rescue the section’s structural critique. That move is methodologically sound and substantively closes the c3 opening.

The two moderate holes I found are NOT in the structural argument — they are in the surface phrasing. Hole 1 (‘same line’) is a literal misstatement that a careful defender will catch by viewing source on the dome page. The fix is mechanical (‘same Tier 1 block’) and the structural point survives. Hole 2 (single-station vs ≥3-station) is a category mismatch: the dome’s rule is explicitly multi-station, the preempting evidence (BOU-2017) is single-station. The fix is a one-sentence pointer to the Meza et al. multi-station INTERMAGNET ensemble already cited two paragraphs earlier in the section. With both fixes, the paragraph is bulletproof.

Advocate Mode — strongest defender comeback: ‘your paragraph misstates the structure of my page (line vs block) and conflates single-station BOU-2017 with my multi-station coherence requirement. Both are sloppy reads of my published rule.’ This is a real opening but it does NOT save the underlying claim. With Hole 1 and Hole 2 fixes, the defender has nothing to grab.

Cross-WIN consistency: the new paragraph is compatible with the WIN-010 eclipse cluster verdict, the WIN-068 honest-caveat framing in the dome’s own text, the §4.2.5 BOU-input critique, and the §4.2.3 hedging-structure argument. No internal anchor links broken (all three #p4-423, #p4-425, #p4-427 still resolve in part4.html).

Quantitative verification: I fetched predictions.html fresh on 2026-04-30 and confirmed both quoted strings are present at lines 113 (Globe prediction) and 125 (Tier 1 falsification). The c3 evidence (lines 113 and 125 from 2026-04-28) survives unchanged. The 12-line gap and intervening station-priority table mean the ‘same line’ phrasing is factually wrong; the ‘same Tier 1 block’ framing is factually right.

Stronger argument the section could absorb: the dome’s OWN amplitude prediction on predictions.html line 110 (-17 to -21 nT) sits inside the Chapman 5–25 nT band cited two paragraphs earlier in our section. So the dome’s sole genuine discriminator is contact-phase TIMING (C1/C4 peaks vs totality maximum), not amplitude. The current section spends most of its prose on amplitude. Adding a sentence acknowledging that the timing argument is the only real test would sharpen the section materially. This is a separate enhancement, not a fix to EXP-275 itself — file as a follow-up issue if desired.

citations_unchecked: BOU 2017 ‘Chapman-pattern coherence with contact times’ — specific empirical claim is not directly cited; presumed to follow from the INTERMAGNET corpus already cited in paragraph 4 but not stated explicitly. See Hole 3.

summary_for_decider: EXP-275 closed the c3 H2 hole (ISS-1672) substantively — the predictions.html three-tier protocol retreat is now blocked by the dome’s own published rule. Verdict (Refuted by Data) holds. Two moderate findings remain in the new paragraph itself: (1) ‘same predictions.html line’ phrasing is factually wrong (lines 113 vs 125 — file as ISS-NEW, 3-word fix to ‘same Tier 1 block’); (2) single-station BOU-2017 evidence does not match the rule’s explicit ≥3-station criterion (file as ISS-NEW, 1-sentence add referencing Meza et al. 2021 multi-station INTERMAGNET ensemble already cited in paragraph 4). Three minor findings (uncited Chapman-coherence claim, slight overclaim on trilemma generalization, doubly-negated phrasing) are polish-grade. recommended_action: minor_edit. The section is in a defensible state; the two moderate fixes would harden it further.
